steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The decisive clue is Jack’s exaggerated line: “It is my belief you would carry a leaky pail on your head in place of a hat, that way you’d not be without some cause of complaining.” This sarcastically implies that Bartley always finds something to complain about, regardless of reality.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content:
D captures Jack’s sarcastic claim that Bartley is perpetually ready to find fault.

A and B misrepresent the dynamic—Jack isn’t worried about Bartley’s weather knowledge, and there’s no evidence Bartley wants to help with hay.

C conflates weather-predicting accuracy with Jack’s point about complaining, which the text never ties to weather-judging skill.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
